name: snmp
explanation: SNMP needs at minimum the device IP and a credential: a community string (v1/v2c) or a v3 user with auth/priv keys. Community strings could occasionally be guessed (`public`/`private`) but production devices have unique strings, and v3 is the modern default. The integration has its own network-discovery feature (`network_address` + profile auto-detection) but it still needs a credential to authenticate. Classification is impossible for credential discovery; SNMP-internal device discovery is orthogonal.
notes: SNMP has its own per-device autodiscovery (network_address + profiles) — that's not the same as auto-config feasibility for the check. Network sweeps still require a credential.
